headlines.title: Artemis II blasts ever closer to the far side of the Moon
headlines.description: The mission's last, big push on its lunar journey takes humans out of the Earth's orbit for the first time since 1972.

headlines.title: M&S boss calls for more action on crime and abuse of staff
headlines.description: Thinus Keeve's comments come days after an M&S store was targeted during disorder in south London.

headlines.title: Trump removes US Attorney General Pam Bondi
headlines.description: Bondi's time as top US law enforcement officer was overshadowed by her handling of the Epstein files.

headlines.title: Crisis staff found 'unforgivable scene' at convicted undertaker's funeral home
headlines.description: An international crisis team was drafted in after police raided Hull's Legacy funeral home.

headlines.title: Major Euston rail disruption across Easter weekend
headlines.description: West Coast Main Line services will not run to London Euston station for six days from Good Friday.

headlines.title: Marmalade to be relabelled under post-Brexit food deal
headlines.description: The breakfast favourite will be legally renamed when Britain aligns with new EU labelling rules.

headlines.title: Police offered support to tackle rising threats against MPs
headlines.description: Reports of crimes against MPs have more than doubled since 2019, reaching almost 1,000 last year.

headlines.title: Myanmar's coup leader who set off a brutal civil war becomes president
headlines.description: General Min Aung Hlaing has been chosen as the next president by the newly-elected parliament.

headlines.title: Judge dismisses most of Blake Lively's claims in harassment lawsuit against Baldoni
headlines.description: The judge left in place three allegations against Baldoni, meaning the civil trial will go ahead next month.

headlines.title: Prince William praises £20m milestone for Bowelbabe fund
headlines.description: The Bowelbabe fund, set up by Dame Deborah James in 2022, helps to support Cancer Research UK.

headlines.title: 'Trust us, you look amazing': Artemis II astronauts speak from space
headlines.description: The crew on board the Artemis II spoke by video link, sharing messages for their families and experiences of the trip so far.

headlines.title: What nearly went wrong on Nasa's space mission - and what still could
headlines.description: The Artemis II crew are now hurtling around the Earth's orbit, carrying out final tests and checks before they head towards the Moon.

headlines.title: Why isn't Artemis II landing on the Moon?
headlines.description: The US is aiming to be the first country to land humans on the lunar surface again by 2028 - so what's this mission for?

headlines.title: 'I ended up paying £500': Your subscription trap stories
headlines.description: Many readers have experienced the ordeal of cancelling a subscription.

headlines.title: The Papers: UK's 'most evil funeral director' and 'Back to the moon'
headlines.description: A funeral director pleading guilty to preventing lawful and decent burial dominates Friday's papers.

headlines.title: Weekly quiz: Why did bananas cause a stir in Orkney?
headlines.description: How much attention did you pay to what happened in the world over the past seven days?

headlines.title: 'I haven't slept for days': Iranians describe mounting desperation after a month of war
headlines.description: Ordinary people from various parts of Iran describe expanding strikes, economic pain and fear of repression.

headlines.title: Arlo Parks: 'I got out of my head and into my body'
headlines.description: The singer-songwriter found herself on the dancefloor. On a new album, she asks us to join her.

headlines.title: 'We fell off the face of the earth': Dad and daughter raced across world but had to keep it secret
headlines.description: Molly Clifford and her father are part of this year's line up for the BBC's Race Across the World.

headlines.title: The thorny issues facing F1 over racing, qualifying and safety
headlines.description: Andrew Benson analyses what we have learned during the first month of the new F1 rules, and the problems that need to be addressed.

headlines.title: Ros Atkins on…the cost of the Iran war
headlines.description: The BBC's Analysis Editor Ros Atkins takes a closer look at US costs as it continues its war with with Iran.

headlines.title: 'Be serious... don't speak every day': Macron criticises Trump approach to Iran war
headlines.description: The comments were an apparent jab at the US leader's sometimes contradictory stance on the US-Israeli war against Iran.

headlines.title: Watch: Trump's shifting deadlines for ending Iran war
headlines.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent analyses the US President's latest White House address.

headlines.title: Iran's focus on survival means same regime still firmly in place
headlines.description: Tehran is showing resilience despite Trump's claims of regime change, writes BBC Persian editor Amir Azimi.

headlines.title: Who is Christopher LaNeve, set to lead the US army?
headlines.description: He will replace Randy George, who was asked to step down from his post by Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth.

headlines.title: BBC boss Tim Davie says it was 'very clear' Scott Mills had to go
headlines.description: Outgoing BBC boss Davie says on his final day Scott Mills' sacking "was a real shock to the organisation".

headlines.title: Funeral fraudster who kept bodies and ashes was 'living beyond his means', ex-worker says
headlines.description: Robert Bush conned the bereaved while living in luxury and enjoying foreign holidays.

headlines.title: 'Unbelievable!': Backshall overjoyed at rare sighting of orcas
headlines.description: Steve Backshall spotted two of the UK's last surviving orcas which he described as one of his "greatest British wildlife moments".

headlines.title: Tiger Woods crash bodycam footage released by police
headlines.description: Body camera footage showing Tiger Woods straight after he clipped a truck and rolled his car in Florida last month is released by police.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: NASA Returns To The Moon (And Beyond)
headlines.description: Nasa launch first crewed mission around the moon in 50 years.

headlines.title: Emma Stone and Ryan Gosling star in an award-winning musical
headlines.description: Exploring the joy and pain of pursuing your dreams

headlines.title: The thorny issues facing F1 over racing, qualifying and safety
headlines.description: Andrew Benson analyses what we have learned during the first month of the new F1 rules, and the problems that need to be addressed.

headlines.title: Woods crash bodycam footage released by police
headlines.description: Body camera footage showing Tiger Woods straight after he clipped a truck and rolled his car in Florida last month is released by police.

headlines.title: 'Only Lauren can match her' - Price in talks to fight Shields
headlines.description: Welterweight champion Lauren Price is in discussions to fight undisputed heavyweight champion Claressa Shields at middleweight.

headlines.title: What De Zerbi needs to do to turn Tottenham around
headlines.description: Tony Pulis explores why Tottenham are in such a mess and what their new manager Roberto de Zerbi might do to change things as they seek to stay in the Premier League.

headlines.title: Hurtling towards relegation - but can Port Vale shock Chelsea in FA Cup?
headlines.description: Six thousand Port Vale fans will travel to Chelsea on Saturday to cheer on the struggling League One side in the quarter-finals of the FA Cup.

headlines.title: Sutton's FA Cup predictions v boxer Richard Riakporhe
headlines.description: Chris Sutton takes on boxer Richard Riakporhe plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this weekend's FA Cup quarter-finals.

headlines.title: Van Veen says Littler 'out of order' as Price wins in Manchester
headlines.description: Gerywn Price secures victory in Manchester after Gian van Veen accuses Luke Littler of being "out of order" on night nine of the Premier League.
